title: Intense Fermented Ethiopian Daikon Misir Wot
description: A traditional Ethiopian dish featuring fermented daikon, made with tadka for added flavor, this recipe is FODMAP-friendly and perfect for a date night.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the cubed daikon radish and enough water to cover it, then let it ferment at room temperature for 3 days.
Drain and rinse the fermented daikon radish, then set it aside.
In a large pot, heat the oil over medium heat, then add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the berbere spice, niter kibbeh, salt, and black pepper, then cook for 1 minute.
Add the red lentils and 2 cups of water to the pot, then bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
Add the fermented daikon radish to the pot and stir to combine.
Simmer the mixture for an additional 10 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together.
